replenishment_policy.instruction: When the todo queue is low, add small Claude Code bridge investigation, proxy, test, or documentation tasks. Research and compatibility tasks may also add follow-up tasks for concrete findings from code inspection, tests, CI logs, docs gaps, or captured live smoke results even when the queue is not low. Prefer three-layer bridge stabilization, Notion persona leakage prevention, tool-result continuation, JSON tool-call mode, session recovery, and golden transcript coverage over broad feature work. Treat dashboard, registration, generic config, OpenCode, OpenAI-compatible, and workflow tasks as secondary unless they directly support a reproduced Claude Code bridge failure. Never create tasks that require secrets, production account data, deployment changes, or workflow permission changes.

autonomous_loop_policy.selection_rule: Choose the exact requested task_id when provided; otherwise choose the first todo task whose risk is low or medium. Todo order should keep Claude Code bridge investigation and regression tasks ahead of dashboard, registration, generic config, OpenCode, OpenAI-compatible, and workflow tasks unless a concrete bridge failure requires those areas.
autonomous_loop_policy.anti_stall_rule: Never ask the user what to do next while safe low or medium risk tasks can be selected or generated.
autonomous_loop_policy.failure_rule: If CI fails because of the current change, fix it before opening or updating the PR. If CI fails for an unrelated reason, add a follow-up task and explain the limitation in the PR body.
autonomous_loop_policy.discovery_rule: For Claude Code bridge research, compatibility, and test tasks, add low/medium-risk follow-up todo tasks for concrete gaps discovered during the work even when the todo queue is not below the replenishment threshold. Prefer findings about Notion persona leakage, tool-call refusal, JSON tool-call mode loss, tool-result continuation, session retry/recovery, final-answer mode, and realistic read/edit/test/finalize transcripts. Keep generated tasks non-duplicative, scoped to one PR, below max_todo_tasks, and backed by allowed_paths plus acceptance criteria.

tasks.title: Prevent Claude Code requests from leaking Notion persona behavior
tasks.description: Add focused regression coverage and bridge fixes so Claude and Claude Code style coding requests keep external assistant instructions and are not answered as Notion workspace/page/document operations. The proxy should preserve coding-assistant behavior while still using Notion as the upstream transport.
acceptance: Tests cover a Claude Code-like request with coding-assistant system instructions through the Anthropic Messages path.
Tests cover an OpenAI-compatible coding-assistant request that must not be transformed into a Notion workspace/page/document style instruction.
Existing Notion upstream transport behavior remains compatible.
No tests call live RDSH, Notion, Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, or GitHub APIs.
Docs explain that Notion persona leakage in Claude/Claude Code requests is a proxy compatibility bug.
If a live behavior check is needed, it is left to the RDSH Live Smoke workflow using the RDSH_API_KEY secret.

tasks.title: Route OpenAI-compatible reasoning_effort to configured model aliases
tasks.description: Teach the OpenAI-compatible chat completions and responses bridges to accept reasoning_effort from clients such as OpenCode. If model_map contains an alias '<model>-<effort>', route the request to that alias before resolving the Notion internal model id. If no alias exists, keep the original model and do not fail the request.
acceptance: OpenAI chat requests accept both reasoning_effort and reasoningEffort inputs without JSON decode errors.
OpenAI responses requests accept reasoning_effort when present.
model='opus-4.8' with reasoning_effort='high' routes through alias 'opus-4.8-high' when that alias exists in model_map.
If the alias is absent, behavior remains backward compatible.
Go tests cover alias routing and fallback behavior.
Docs explain how OpenCode variants can use reasoningEffort with RDSH.
Do not change /v1/models behavior or handler_models_test.go; public model alias discovery is covered by the separate public-models-include-configured-aliases task.

tasks.title: Expose configured friendly model aliases in the public models list
tasks.description: Improve /v1/models so configured model_map aliases that are safe public names can appear alongside account-discovered models. This helps OpenAI-compatible clients discover manually configured aliases such as opus-4.8-high.
acceptance: /v1/models includes configured friendly aliases without duplicates.
Internal Notion model ids are not exposed when no friendly alias exists.
Existing account-discovered model behavior remains compatible.
Tests cover duplicate handling and fallback behavior.

tasks.title: Add regression tests for OpenAI-compatible streaming translation
tasks.description: Add focused tests around OpenAI-compatible streaming event translation for text, tool calls, and reasoning summary events using local fixtures only.
acceptance: Tests cover text delta streaming behavior.
Tests cover tool call delta behavior where existing code supports it.
Tests cover reasoning summary event translation from Anthropic thinking blocks.
No tests call external APIs.

tasks.title: Add regression coverage for OpenAI and Anthropic error normalization
tasks.description: Add tests that verify unsupported options, malformed inputs, and upstream failures return stable error shapes for OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic-compatible clients.
acceptance: Malformed OpenAI-compatible requests return stable invalid_request_error style responses.
Malformed Anthropic-compatible requests return stable error responses.
Unsupported options are documented when intentionally ignored.
Tests use local httptest/fakes only.

tasks.title: Document model_map aliases for OpenCode and OpenAI-compatible clients
tasks.description: Document how to create friendly model aliases in model_map and how OpenAI-compatible clients can select them directly or via variants.
acceptance: Docs include an example for opus-4.8-high mapping to a Notion internal id.
Docs explain that clients may select aliases as model ids.
Docs warn not to commit real config.yaml or account data.
Example config remains secret-free.

tasks.title: Improve dashboard visibility for configured model aliases
tasks.description: Make the dashboard model/admin view clearly show configured friendly aliases separately from account-discovered internal models.
acceptance: Dashboard or admin API distinguishes configured aliases from discovered models.
No account secrets or internal tokens are displayed.
Frontend build passes.
Backend tests cover the admin payload shape when changed.

tasks.title: Add test coverage for publicModelID normalization
tasks.description: Add focused tests to verify that publicModelID normalizes model names properly and that buildPublicModels uses normalized names correctly.
acceptance: Tests cover publicModelID behavior.
Tests verify normalizeModelName interactions.

tasks.title: Add test coverage for proxy timeout scenarios
tasks.description: Add focused tests verifying that the proxy behaves correctly when upstream requests timeout or fail with network errors.
acceptance: Tests cover timeout handling.
Tests simulate network failures.

tasks.title: Add validation tests for model map configuration
tasks.description: Add tests to ensure that the model map correctly normalizes alias names and handles missing or empty keys.
acceptance: Tests cover alias normalization.
Tests handle edge cases for model map.

tasks.title: Build a global Claude Code agent-loop regression matrix
tasks.description: Research Claude Code style agent loops at the behavior level and add durable offline coverage for Notion persona leakage, tool-result continuation, and lost coding intent. The work should model current Claude Code surfaces such as CLAUDE.md instructions, slash commands, hooks, MCP-style tools, subagent/delegated prompts, non-interactive automation, and multi-turn read/edit/test/finalize loops. If the investigation finds additional safe gaps that do not fit this PR, add follow-up todo tasks with concrete allowed_paths and acceptance criteria.
acceptance: Docs define a behavior-level Claude Code compatibility matrix rather than only one-off phrase fixes.
Offline tests or fixtures cover at least three realistic Claude Code style transcript categories, including a multi-turn tool-result continuation.
Regression coverage detects or prevents Notion persona leakage/refusal language in coding-assistant paths where local fakes make that practical.
The work preserves existing Notion upstream transport behavior and does not add live network calls to unit tests.
Any discovered compatibility gaps outside this PR are added as low/medium-risk follow-up tasks with allowed_paths and acceptance criteria.

tasks.title: Add coverage for request logging middleware
tasks.description: Add tests verifying that HTTP request logging captures essential fields without leaking sensitive data.
acceptance: Tests cover request logging.
Tests verify sensitive data is omitted.

tasks.title: Add tests for dashboard model alias visibility payload shape
tasks.description: Add tests verifying that the admin payload distinguishes configured aliases from discovered models correctly.
acceptance: Tests verify the admin models payload shape.
Tests ensure configured aliases are correctly marked in the response.

tasks.title: Add dashboard authentication handler tests
tasks.description: Add tests verifying that dashboard login endpoints accept correct passwords and reject incorrect ones.
acceptance: Tests verify login with correct password.
Tests verify login with incorrect password.

tasks.title: Add configuration parsing tests
tasks.description: Add tests to verify that config.yaml files are parsed correctly and missing fields are handled properly.
acceptance: Tests verify parsing valid configuration.
Tests verify defaults are applied to missing fields.

tasks.title: Add regression tests for streaming tool-call events
tasks.description: Add tests to ensure Anthropic and OpenAI compatible proxy streaming handles tool call chunks accurately without splitting JSON keys.
acceptance: Tests verify tool call parameter streaming chunks.
Tests use local fixtures only.

tasks.title: Add tests for streaming error handling
tasks.description: Add focused tests verifying that the stream transcoder gracefully handles partial or broken JSON frames.
acceptance: Tests cover truncated stream frames.
Tests cover invalid JSON in event data.

tasks.title: Document model map fallback behavior
tasks.description: Add missing documentation describing how model mapping falls back to the original model name if no alias matches.
acceptance: Docs explain fallback behavior when an alias is missing.
Example configuration reflects fallback logic.

tasks.title: Add tests for debug logging toggles
tasks.description: Add tests to ensure debug logging can be toggled via settings.
acceptance: Tests cover enabling/disabling debug logs.
Tests verify log output.

tasks.title: Add tests for proxy health endpoint
tasks.description: Add tests to verify the /health endpoint correctly reports system status.
acceptance: Tests cover GET /health endpoint.
Tests verify JSON payload structure.

tasks.title: Add test coverage for invalid tool types
tasks.description: Add tests to ensure the proxy correctly handles unknown tool types in the chat completions bridge.
acceptance: Tests verify correct error shape for unsupported tool types.
Tests verify correct status code for unsupported tool types.

tasks.title: Add tests for empty content blocks
tasks.description: Add tests to ensure the proxy correctly handles empty content blocks from the streaming API.
acceptance: Tests cover empty text delta in streaming transcoder.
Tests cover empty thinking block in streaming transcoder.

tasks.title: Build Claude Code bridge failure taxonomy fixtures
tasks.description: Document and test the main failure classes in the three-layer Claude Code bridge: Notion persona leakage, tool-call refusal, JSON tool-call mode loss, tool-result continuation loss, and final-answer identity drift. Use offline fixtures or local fake responses only.
acceptance: Docs define a concise taxonomy of observed Claude Code bridge failure classes.
Offline tests or fixtures cover at least three distinct failure classes.
Tests assert that bridge outputs do not contain Notion workspace/page/document refusal language for coding-agent paths.
No tests call live Notion, RDSH, Anthropic, OpenAI, GitHub, Google, or Microsoft APIs.
Any additional concrete gaps discovered are added as bounded follow-up tasks.

tasks.title: Add Claude Code tool-result continuation regression coverage
tasks.description: Add realistic multi-turn Claude Code transcript coverage where a tool_use result is followed by either the next tool call or a final __done__ answer without drifting into Notion persona behavior.
acceptance: Tests model at least one read/edit/test or read/command/finalize Claude Code style loop.
Continuation prompts preserve coding intent after tool_result messages.
Final answers use __done__ handling or equivalent final-answer mode without returning a fake tool call.
Regression checks fail on Notion persona or page/workspace/document refusal language in coding-agent paths.
Existing session fingerprint and recovery behavior remains backward compatible.

tasks.title: Harden JSON tool-call extraction for Claude Code bridge responses
tasks.description: Improve and test extraction of JSON tool-call and __done__ payloads from bridge responses that include harmless preambles, code fences, or whitespace while still rejecting unsafe or ambiguous Notion-style prose.
acceptance: Tests cover JSON tool calls wrapped in common preambles or code fences.
Tests cover __done__ payload extraction for final answers.
Ambiguous prose or Notion refusal text is not accepted as a valid tool call.
Streaming and non-streaming paths remain compatible with existing tests.
Docs describe the accepted bridge response shapes and rejected failure shapes.

tasks.title: Preserve Claude Code coding intent while stripping unsafe control text
tasks.description: Add regression coverage for CLAUDE.md-like instructions, slash-command prompts, hook-style reminders, and MCP-style tool context so XML/control stripping removes dangerous prompt-killers without deleting the coding intent needed by the bridge.
acceptance: Tests cover at least three Claude Code surface types: project instructions, slash-command style text, hooks, MCP context, or subagent-style prompts.
Sanitization removes known response-killing control text while preserving the actionable coding request.
Tests detect loss of coding intent that would cause a Notion workspace-style answer.
No test requires the full Claude Code system prompt or live Claude Code execution.
Docs explain which instruction categories are preserved, summarized, or stripped.

tasks.title: Add tests for nested object handling in tool parameters
tasks.description: Add tests verifying that the proxy correctly parses and passes along deeply nested object structures in tool calls.
acceptance: Tests cover tool calls with deeply nested JSON objects.
Tests verify the nested structure is preserved accurately.

tasks.title: Add tests for configuration validation logic
tasks.description: Add tests verifying that invalid configurations are rejected appropriately.
acceptance: Tests cover invalid config loading.
Tests verify error messages.

tasks.title: Add tests for dashboard specific routing
tasks.description: Add tests verifying that the dashboard handler correctly routes API vs static file requests.
acceptance: Tests verify routing to API paths.
Tests verify routing to static file paths.

tasks.title: Add test coverage for large streaming chunks
tasks.description: Add tests to ensure the streaming transcoder properly handles very large text deltas without truncating data.
acceptance: Tests verify correct handling of unusually large string payloads.
Output string should match the exact length of the concatenated chunks.

tasks.title: Verify dashboard logout routing
tasks.description: Add tests verifying that logging out correctly invalidates the session and responds appropriately for the frontend.
acceptance: Tests verify /auth/logout clears the session.

tasks.title: Expand test coverage for config environment variable overrides
tasks.description: Add tests verifying that environment variables can override nested configuration properties, such as the Notion API endpoint.
acceptance: Tests verify env vars correctly take precedence over file-based config.
Tests verify defaults are applied correctly when neither exist.

tasks.title: Add test for system message omission
tasks.description: Add tests to ensure the proxy behaves predictably when requests contain no system messages, ensuring compatibility with strict models.
acceptance: Tests verify request transformation when system message is absent.
Anthropic request must correctly omit system field if empty.

tasks.title: Add tests for model alias case sensitivity
tasks.description: Add tests ensuring that incoming model aliases are handled case-insensitively, preventing mapping misses.
acceptance: Tests verify case-insensitive alias lookups.
Tests verify original model name is used if mapping fails entirely.
